title: Unsigned requests accepted: no sig = full access to Follow/Undo handlers
description:
The Signature header is optional. server.rs:456 checks `if let Some(sig_header) = headers.get("signature")` — if the header is absent, the block is skipped entirely and the activity is processed without any authentication.

An unauthenticated attacker can:
1. POST a Follow activity as any actor URI (e.g., `"actor": "https://mastodon.social/users/victim"`) with no Signature header. Broadside will insert a follower row and send an Accept to that actor's inbox.
2. POST an Undo{Follow} to silently delete any row from the followers table by setting `object.actor` to the target URI — also with no signature.

Both operations permanently mutate state. The comment at server.rs:678 acknowledges the Undo issue but the comment says it's 'safe only after signature verification is implemented' — yet Undo is live and reachable right now with no signature required.

Impact: follower table stuffing (spam amplifier), follower table wipe (DoS of delivery list), impersonation of remote actors.
close_reason: All pentest fixes: require Signature header, verify Digest, enforce actor-keyId match, Date freshness, body size limit 256KB, webhook key via header, decompression bomb limits, symlink rejection, redirect following disabled, delivery SSRF recheck, backslash escape handling
